entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: An African grass grown for food, feed, fibre, and fuel, with a 730-megabase genome.

entities.name: RICE
entities.description: A crop used for comparative analysis of genome size, gene order, and density against sorghum.

entities.name: WHOLE-GENOME SHOTGUN SEQUENCE
entities.description: A sequencing method used to place genes in their chromosomal context in the sorghum genome.

entities.name: GENETIC RECOMBINATION
entities.description: A process largely confined to about one-third of the sorghum genome.

entities.name: RETROTRANSPOSON ACCUMULATION
entities.description: The buildup of retrotransposons in heterochromatin, contributing to larger genome size.

entities.name: HETEROCHROMATIN
entities.description: Recombinationally recalcitrant regions of the genome where retrotransposons accumulate.

entities.name: GENOME SIZE
entities.description: The total amount of DNA in a genome, which is 75% larger in sorghum than in rice.

entities.name: PALAEOPOLYPLOIDIZATION
entities.description: An ancient genome duplication event occurring approximately 70 million years ago.

entities.name: CONCERTED EVOLUTION
entities.description: An evolutionary process that makes duplicated chromosomal segments appear relatively young.

entities.name: MICRORNA DUPLICATIONS
entities.description: Recent duplications of microRNA genes that may contribute to drought tolerance.

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of sorghum to withstand drought conditions.

relationships.description: used to analyze the genome of
